page: Amazon Web Services r5d.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 3.1 GHz. The instance provides 8 vCPUs across 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Additionally, the instance includes 300 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across most standard operations, with some single-threaded and multi-core limitations. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression are categorized in the bottom 25%, reflecting weaker raw compute scaling. However, the instance achieves average results in Geekbench single-core tests, Passmark CPU scores, and Redis SET operations, which reached 6.82 million operations per second. Memory bandwidth tests also show average performance, with read-only small block cached speeds at 388.87 GB/sec and large block uncached write speeds at 38.68 GB/sec.

The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage alongside a high memory capacity makes this instance class suitable for workloads requiring fast local scratch space and large memory footprints, such as relational databases, caching layers, and medium-scale data processing. However, users face a tradeoff in raw compute power, as demonstrated by poor to weak performance in LLM inference tasks and lower-tier multi-core Geekbench scores. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r5d.2xlarge offers a balanced resource density for memory-bound and local-storage-dependent applications without the overhead of dedicated high-compute hardware.
description: Amazon Web Services r5d.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory, powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 300 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network speed of 2 Gbps. Benchmark results place the instance in the average tier for memory bandwidth, Passmark CPU, and Redis SET operations, though it exhibits weaker performance in single-core stress-ng and multi-core Geekbench tests. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, combined with local NVMe storage, makes this instance suitable for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and caching systems that require fast local temporary storage, despite its moderate compute performance.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5d.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and 300 GB local NVMe SSD storage, suitable for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5d.2xlarge is an 8-vCPU memory-optimized instance with 64 GB RAM and 300 GB NVMe SSD storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized instance featuring local NVMe SSD storage and balanced memory bandwidth for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores
64.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
300 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for fast temporary data access
Average performance in Redis SET operations and Passmark database tests
Weaker single-core and multi-core compute scaling in heavy processing benchmarks
Best suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and data processing
